_comment: Zero-Python Unit 5: all hook commands route through bin/hook-client (Node client preferred - no node_modules needed; Python client fallback; '{}' floor) and SessionStart routes through bin/ensure-coordinator-dispatch (coordinator_backend selection: env > .coherence/coordinator_backend > python default). Command-type hooks per Phase E.0 probe 2A: CC's schema validator rejects env-var URLs, so HTTP-type hooks are structurally infeasible; the client reads .coherence/server.pid itself.

PreToolUse._comment: KTD-N H4 mitigation — the model routes around a denied/warned Read via `bash cat plan.md`. Without a Bash PreToolUse matcher, that routing pattern silently bypasses the stale-read warning surface, leaving the plugin's coherence guarantee intact only for the literal Read tool. The pre-bash client subcommand extracts tracked-artifact paths from the Bash command string and forwards them to /hooks/pre-bash on the coordinator.

KTD-N H4 mitigation — Grep is another bypass route: a user-initiated or model-initiated grep over a directory containing tracked artifacts (CLAUDE.md, plan.md, etc.) should surface stale-read warnings for artifacts the session hasn't freshened since peer commits. The pre-grep client subcommand extracts the search root and forwards to /hooks/pre-grep on the coordinator, which enumerates registry-known tracked artifacts under the prefix.

SubagentStop._comment: SB-25 Unit 4: release the SUBAGENT identity's uncommitted grants when it stops. The subcommand requires the payload's agent_id (validated against the subagent-id charset) - absent OR malformed means no-op {}, never a parent release. Backstop: the Python coordinator's heartbeat/idle sweep reclaims a grant if SubagentStop is missed; the Node coordinator has no sweep yet, so on the Node backend a missed SubagentStop holds the grant until coordinator restart (tracked follow-up).
